channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sky cruising: Africa’s airlines juggling record‑high and volatile jet fuel prices

item.title: Young man dies after stabbing incident in Caloocan City
item.description: A 22-year-old man died after being repeatedly stabbed during a violent incident in Caloocan City. According to initial police reports, the victim was attacked late at night in a residential area by an...

item.title: Why investing in maternal nutrition is crucial for Africa's future
item.description: In a critical moment for East Africa, almost one out of every three children under five is affected by stunting due to malnutrition, compromising their futures. As the International Maternal Newborn H...

item.title: Ex-Billabong Executives Propose Dividing KMD Brands to Acquire Rip Curl
item.description: Former Billabong executives have proposed a major restructuring of KMD Brands, the company behind Kathmandu, in a bid to acquire Rip Curl, a surfwear brand with more than 50 years of history. The prop...

item.title: Iran warns of strikes on Gulf energy and desalination plants if US hits its power grid amid ongoing war
item.description: In the escalating US-Israel war against Iran, now in its fourth week starting from February 28, 2026, Iran has issued a strong warning of retaliation. On March 22, Tehran stated it would target energy...

item.description: The UK Government has announced plans to construct seven new towns across England as part of Labour's pledge to deliver 1.5 million homes during this parliamentary term. Each town will offer at least ...

item.description: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk returned to Leh on March 22, 2026, after nearly six months of detention under the National Security Act (NSA), which was revoked by the Centre. He received a warm welco...

item.title: Durban City secure important win over Richards Bay in local derby
item.description: Durban City extended their impressive home form with a narrow 1-0 victory over Richards Bay in a thrilling KwaZulu-Natal derby at Chatsworth Stadium on Sunday. The match, part of the Betway Premiershi...

item.title: Kenyan Athletes Face Challenges at World Athletics Indoor Championships in Poland
item.description: Kenyan athletes had a tough time at the World Athletics Indoor Championships held in Torun, Poland, with no medals secured in three finals on the penultimate day of the competition. Mercy Oketch, Bria...

item.title: Australia’s Struggle to Confront its Role in the Christchurch Terror Attack

item.title: First person charged under new law criminalising entry to football matches without a ticket
item.description: A new piece of legislation, the Unauthorised Entry to Football Matches Act 2026, came into force on Sunday 22 March 2026, making it a criminal offence in England and Wales to enter or attempt to enter...

item.title: South Africa issues over 3 000 gambling licences yearly with bookmaker surge prompting regulation worries
item.description: South Africa’s gambling sector continues to expand rapidly, with more than 3 000 licences issued to operators each year. Official figures show 3 135 licences were granted in the 2024/2025 financial ye...

item.title: President Tinubu Congratulates Tony Elumelu on 63rd Birthday, Recognises His Impact on African Entrepreneurship
